entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench, a crop evaluated for nitrogen use efficiency in sole and intercropping systems

entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Cajanus cajan (L.) Millsp., a legume crop evaluated for nitrogen fixation and use efficiency in sole and intercropping systems

entities.name: NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The biological process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into a usable form, specifically by pigeonpea

entities.name: NITROGEN FERTILIZER USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which a crop utilizes applied nitrogen fertilizer

entities.name: DRY MATTER PRODUCTION
entities.description: The total biomass produced by the crops, used as a measure of productivity

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area

entities.name: LAND EQUIVALENT RATIO
entities.description: A metric used to determine the productivity advantage of intercropping over sole cropping

entities.name: AREA-TIME EQUIVALENT RATIO
entities.description: A metric used to determine the productivity advantage of intercropping over sole cropping

entities.name: 15N-LABELING
entities.description: An isotopic labeling method used to estimate nitrogen fertilizer use efficiency and nitrogen fixation

entities.name: NATURAL ABUNDANCE METHOD
entities.description: A method using naturally occurring nitrogen isotopes to estimate nitrogen fixation

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two or more crops simultaneously on the same field

entities.name: SOLE CROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing a single crop on a field

entities.name: ALFISOL
entities.description: A specific type of soil on which the field experiment was conducted

entities.name: INDIAN SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The geographic region where the study was performed

entities.name: SYMBIOTICALLY FIXED N
entities.description: Nitrogen fixed from the atmosphere through a symbiotic relationship between the plant and bacteria

entities.name: SOIL N
entities.description: Nitrogen naturally available in the soil

entities.name: FERTILIZER N
entities.description: Nitrogen provided to the crops through chemical fertilizers

entities.name: LAND PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The overall output and efficiency of land use in cropping systems
